description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rough, natural surface and a brownish coloration. The stone has a somewhat concave depression on one side, giving it a saddle-like appearance, and its edges are uneven and jagged. On one face, there is a small, rectangular label with handwritten text, suggesting it may have been cataloged or collected for study. The stone's thickness is minimal compared to its width and length, making it appear almost flat when viewed from the side. Its surface texture is coarse, with visible pitting and natural weathering marks. The coloration varies subtly, with hints of reddish and yellowish tones blending into the dominant brown.
description: A rough, brown, irregularly shaped stone with a concave depression and a catalog label on one side. Its surface is coarse and weathered, with jagged edges and subtle color variations.
description_view_0: The stone appears wide and shallow with a pronounced concave depression and rough, uneven edges.
description_view_1: From the side, the stone looks very thin and flat, with its irregular outline visible but little surface detail.
description_view_2: This view shows the opposite face, featuring a small, rectangular label with handwritten text and the same jagged, rough edges.
description_view_3: The stone is again seen from the side, emphasizing its thinness and irregular profile, with the label edge just visible.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a decorative obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: No regular pattern; surface is mottled with natural color variations and pitting, and one side has a man-made label.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible pitting and minor surface erosion indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping features.
